channel.title: briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from briefUpdatesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Penpulimab and lenalidomide in combination with standard of care chemoimmunotherapy demonstrates promising safety and efficacy in diffuse large B-cell lymphoma
item.description: 1. Penpulimab and lenalidomide in combination with rituximab, gemcitabine, and oxaliplatin (R-GemOx regimen) is safe, tolerable, and shows antitumour ...

item.title: Wyoming wants to make its 5-year federal rural health funding last 'forever'
item.description: Wyoming officials say they have a plan to make five years of upcoming grants from a new $50 billion federal rural health program last "forever."

item.title: Locum tenens offers physicians a path to freedom [PODCAST]
item.description: Subscribe to The Podcast by KevinMD. Watch on YouTube. Catch up on old episodes! Pediatrician Trevor Cabrera discusses his article “Locum tenens: Recl...

item.title: Exercise May Improve Survival in a Variety of Cancer Types
item.description: New data suggest that even modest levels of exercise after a cancer diagnosis may benefit survivors of less commonly studied cancers. Medscape Medical...

item.title: FDA's abrupt flip-flop on Moderna's mRNA flu shot highlights growing risks to drug-makers of investing in vaccines
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration's decision, made public on Feb. 10, 2026, to not review an application to approve Moderna's proposed mRNA-based flu v...

item.title: Exercise May Mitigate Risk for Preeclampsia in Pregnancy
item.description: Regular aerobic exercise between pregnancies is linked to improved heart health and a reduced risk for preeclampsia recurring in a subsequent pregnanc...

item.title: New Zealand faces severe shortage of neurologists, study finds
item.description: New Zealand does not have enough neurologists to keep up with the increase in demand for the diagnosis and treatment of conditions such as multiple sc...

item.title: Colorectal screening boosts early diagnoses in first two years, randomized trial suggests
item.description: More cases of colorectal cancer are detected at an early stage with screening. This is according to new research based on data from over 278,000 60-ye...

item.title: New data on spontaneous coronary artery dissection—a common cause of heart attacks in younger women
item.description: New insights into spontaneous coronary artery dissection—a devastating cause of heart attacks in young, healthy patients—were presented at the EAPCI S...

item.title: ACC: Don’t Mandate ECG Screening for Athletes
item.description: ECG screening for athletes finds more cardiovascular problems, but US states should not require it due to potential downsides, ACC says. Medscape Medi...

item.title: Groups Clash Over State Medical Malpractice Reforms

item.title: Dental Disease as a Performance Risk
item.description: Poor oral health in elite athletes, with high caries and periodontal rates, can impair sleep, recovery, and training adaptation. Authors call for rout...

item.title: Artificial intelligence designed drugs Hit 90% Phase I success rate in trials
item.description: 1. AI-native biotechnology firms are reporting Phase I success rates between 80% and 90%, nearly doubling the historical industry average of approxima...

item.title: Understanding the 4 models of health care: Where the U.S. fits
item.description: We are frequently told that our health care system is expensive. However, most of us, including physicians, have no notion about what the health care ...

item.title: What got you here won’t get you there: a physician’s guide to leadership
item.description: You became a physician because you were driven. You studied longer, pushed harder, and tolerated pressure better than most. You needed to be right and...

item.title: The 3-2-1 method: a doctor’s guide to keeping New Year’s resolutions

item.title: Sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitors may improve left ventricular diastolic function in hypertrophic cardiomyopathy
